tasks.text: Replace scalar world AABB loop in RayTraceRowFunc (ray.c:615-618) with AVX2 8-wide rayAABB_invV4_avx2, matching the commented-out code pattern at lines 599-614. Process 8 objects per SIMD iteration, fall back to scalar for remainder.
Copy `rayAABB_invV4_avx2` and `transpose_float3x8_to_soa` from `tests/rayAABB_inv.h` into `object/object.h` immediately after the `rayAABB_inv` function (after line 175).

Modify `RayTraceRowFunc` in `render/cpu/ray.c` (lines 598-632): Add alignas(32) arrays, call `rayAABB_invV4_avx2` for batches of 8, fall back to scalar `rayAABB_inv` for remainder, matching the pattern in the commented-out code at lines 599-614.
Run `makeBench` and `makeFlame` after the code change to validate the performance improvement and verify correctness.
If benchmarks pass and flame graph shows reduced `rayAABB_inv` time, commit the changes and create a PR. If regressed, run `getDiff` and investigate the fallback loop logic.
Check Makefile for AVX2/FMA compiler flags before applying changes
Read rayTriangle in object/object.c to confirm division strategy (where is 1/det computed relative to early-out branches?)
Apply divisionless early-out optimization: compare u/det against threshold becomes compare u against det. Division only for confirmed hits.
Build and bench. Target: >= 2% reduction in avg_ms (< 8.49ms). If regressed, restore and investigate.
Apply divisionless early-out to rayTriangle in object/object.c
Build and bench. Target: >= 2% reduction in avg_ms (< 8.49ms). If regressed, restore and investigate.

notes.text: Baseline bench: 8.66ms avg, 1152 frames/10s. Flame graph shows rayAABB_inv at 8.6% exclusive time as #3 hot function (after rayTriangle 21% and IntersectBVH 22%). The scalar world AABB test in RayTraceRowFunc iterates 1 object at a time. AVX2 version exists in tests/rayAABB_inv.h as rayAABB_invV4_avx2 which processes 8 boxes at once.
[PLAN] Analysis complete. Baseline 8.66ms. Top hotspots identified. Task #1 (AVX2 AABB) is the confirmed next step. No code changes attempted yet. Ready to execute.

Read the AVX2 functions from tests/rayAABB_inv.h (lines 34-87): transpose_float3x8_to_soa and rayAABB_invV4_avx2 process 8 AABBs at once. The insertion point in object/object.h is after line 175 (after scalar rayAABB_inv, before SSE rayAABB_inv_x2_soa). The commented-out code in ray.c:599-614 shows the intended batch loop pattern for RayTraceRowFunc. Need to read the loop body after line 620 to understand the BVH intersect + material code that goes in the inner loop.

[PLAN] I have all information needed. Ready to apply AVX2 AABB change. Need to check build flags for AVX2 support, then apply two changes: (1) add AVX2 functions to object/object.h, (2) modify RayTraceRowFunc loop in ray.c. No more reading — execute now.
Makefile uses -march=native (line 2), which enables AVX2 and FMA3 on modern CPUs. Confirmed: AVX2 intrinsics will compile. Ready to apply both changes now.

AVX2 AABB batching FAILED (-1.2% regression). Root cause: scatter/gather overhead of transposing 8 float3 pairs from scattered objects + loss of early-exit (as bestT shrinks, scalar loop skips many AABB tests cheaply, but SIMD batch processes all 8 before checking). restoreAll called. Abandoning this approach.

NEW PLAN: Target rayTriangle (21% exclusive time, #2 hotspot). Remove division from early-out rejection paths. In Möller–Trumbore, compare u*v_det > 1.0f becomes u > det (avoiding 1/det). Division only needed for confirmed hits. This is surgically targeting the most expensive operation (div) on the most common path (miss for shadow rays, early-out for primary).
Clearing old AVX2 AABB tasks from board and setting new target: rayTriangle division removal.

[PLAN] Targetting rayTriangle division removal. Current baseline 8.66ms avg. The scalar rayTriangle at object/object.c:490 computes 1/a unconditionally at line 498, then immediately branches on u (line 501). Moving the division after u and v early-outs means we only pay the 10-40 cycle div for actual hits (~5% of calls). Target: >= 2% reduction (avg < 8.49ms).

rayTriangle divisionless early-out: SUCCESS. avg_ms 8.659 -> 8.487 (-2.0%), frames 1152 -> 1175 (+2.0%), median_ms 8.617 -> 8.418 (-2.3%). Image MSE 7.67 (acceptable minor difference from fp-reordering). The change removes 1.0f/a division from the common case (miss/early-out) and defers it to only confirmed hits. This surgically targets the #2 hotspot's most expensive instruction.
